other_observers: none that day, Colorado birders had another individual sight the bird two days later in the same area.; Yes
specific_location: Cherry Creek State Park, west side of reservoir, south of mariana, in a grass area some 200 yards away from the water.
description: Predominatly black in color, with slight white coloring in the shoulders, slight white colar on the upper neck. Black Bill, Black feet, black eyes. I would estimate the bird to be about 5 lbs. in size. The amount of white on the bird identified it as a "western race".Behaviors: I spotted the bird on March 17, 2005 around 11:30 am. ,on the west side of Cherry Creek Reservoir, south of the marina. It appeared to be grazing on grass. Bird was grazing with 10 Greater Canadian Geese and one Greater White-Fronted Goose. The birds it was with appeared to be migratatory birds rather than the local geese, they were not displaying any early courtship behavior as the locals are. I stayed in my car and used binoculars, the bird came within 75 feet of me. The Brant was very alert to movement and the environment and stayed loosely with the flock.Call: none givenPlumage: mature
